summary: Data practitioners worldwide feel misunderstood and underappreciated because they're too removed from core business activities, constantly playing defense. Reis argues that data teams need to proactively engage with business initiatives and demonstrate they can get ahead of needs rather than just react to them.
key_points: Data professionals globally share the same problems: feeling misunderstood, underappreciated, and overwhelmed by 'the business'
Data teams are playing defense by default, receiving work rather than driving it
The solution is to proactively integrate into key business initiatives where you can move the needle
Showing you care about the business will make the business care about you
money_quotes: No matter where you go in the data world, the problems are the same.
Too often, data practitioners are far removed from the core parts of the business. Often being on the receiving end, they're playing defense by default.
Sh*t flows downhill, and data teams are famous for their ability to receive sh*t in volumes, often by the truckload.
If you can show that you care about the business, the business will care about you. Respect gets respect.
key_insight: Data teams must shift from passive recipients of business requests to proactive partners who engage with and get ahead of business needs to earn respect and relevance.
